name: networksetup
description: Configuration tool for network settings in System Preferences

subcommands.name: -listnetworkserviceorder
subcommands.description: Displays a list of network services in the order they are contacted for a connection, along with the corresponding port and device for each

subcommands.name: -listallnetworkservices
subcommands.description: Displays a list of all the network services on the server's hardware ports

subcommands.name: -listallhardwareports
subcommands.description: Displays list of hardware ports with corresponding device name and ethernet address

subcommands.name: -detectnewhardware
subcommands.description: Detects new network hardware and creates a default network service on the hardware

subcommands.name: -getmacaddress
subcommands.description: Displays ethernet (or Wi-Fi) address for hardwareport or device specified

subcommands.name: -getcomputername
subcommands.description: Displays the computer name

subcommands.name: -setcomputername
subcommands.description: Sets computer name to <computername>. This name is used by AFP

args.name: computername
args.description: The new name for your computer on the network

subcommands.name: -getinfo
subcommands.description: Displays the IP address, subnet mask, router, and hardware address for the <networkservice> that you specify

subcommands.name: -setmanual
subcommands.description: Set the TCP/IP configuration for <networkservice> to manual with IP address set to <ip>, Subnet Mask set to <subnet>, and Router address set to <router>

args.name: ip
args.description: The IP address to set for the network service

args.name: subnet
args.description: The subnet mask to apply

args.name: router
args.description: The router address to set

subcommands.name: -setdhcp
subcommands.description: Use this command to set the TCP/IP configuration for the specified <networkservice> to use DHCP. The client ID is optional. Specify "Empty" for [clientid] to clear the DHCP client id

args.name: clientid
args.description: Optional DHCP client ID. Use 'Empty' to clear it

subcommands.name: -setbootp
subcommands.description: Use this command to set the TCP/IP configuration for the specified <networkservice> to use BOOTP

subcommands.name: -setmanualwithdhcprouter
subcommands.description: Use this command to specify a manual IP address to use for DHCP for the specified <networkservice>

args.name: ip
args.description: The IP address to set for the network service

subcommands.name: -getadditionalroutes
subcommands.description: Use this command to display the list of additional IPv4 routes configured for the service

subcommands.name: -setadditionalroutes
subcommands.description: Use this command to set the list of IPv4 additional routes configured for the service. Each route is specified as a (destination address, subnet mask, gateway address) tuple. Specifying no tuples clears the list of routes

args.name: dest mask gate
args.description: One or more tuples of (destination address, subnet mask, gateway address). Specify 'Empty' to clear routes

subcommands.name: -setv4off
subcommands.description: Use this command to turn IPv4 off on the specified <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setv6off
subcommands.description: Use this command to turn IPv6 off on the specified <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setv6automatic
subcommands.description: Use this command to set IPv6 to get its addresses automatically for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setv6linklocal
subcommands.description: Use this command to set IPv6 to only use link local for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setv6manual
subcommands.description: Use this command to set IPv6 to get its addresses manually for <networkservice>. Specify the ip address, the prefix length and the router

args.name: ip
args.description: The IPv6 address to assign

args.name: prefixlength
args.description: The prefix length for the IPv6 address (e.g., 64)

args.name: router
args.description: The IPv6 address of the router

subcommands.name: -getv6additionalroutes
subcommands.description: Use this command to display the list of additional IPv6 routes configured for the service

subcommands.name: -setv6additionalroutes
subcommands.description: Use this command to set the list of additional routes configured for the service. Each route is specified as a (destination address, prefix length, gateway address) tuple. Specifying no tuples clears the list of routes

args.name: dest prefixlength gate
args.description: The list of routes, each specified as a tuple of destination address, prefix length, and gateway address. Provide multiple routes or omit this to clear all routes

subcommands.name: -getdnsservers
subcommands.description: Displays DNS info for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setdnsservers
subcommands.description: Use this command to specify the IP addresses of servers you want the specified <networkservice> to use to resolve domain names. You can list any number of servers (replace dns1, dns2, and so on with the IP addresses of domain name servers). If you want to clear all DNS entries for the specified network service, type "empty" in place of the DNS server names

args.name: dns
args.description: IP addresses of DNS servers. Use 'empty' to clear all DNS entries

subcommands.name: -getsearchdomains
subcommands.description: Displays Domain Name info for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setsearchdomains
subcommands.description: Use this command to designate the search domain for the specified <networkservice>. You can list any number of search domains (replace domain1, domain2, and so on with the name of a local domain). If you want to clear all search domain entries for the specified network service, type “empty” in place of the domain name

args.name: domain
args.description: List of search domains. Use 'empty' to clear all existing domains

subcommands.name: -create6to4service
subcommands.description: Use this command to create a new 6 to 4 service with name <newnetworkservicename>

args.name: newnetworkservicename
args.description: The name of the new 6 to 4 service to be created

subcommands.name: -set6to4automatic
subcommands.description: Use this command to set the 6 to 4 service such that it will get the relay address automatically

args.name: newnetworkservicename
args.description: The name of the 6 to 4 service to be configured for automatic relay address retrieval

subcommands.name: -set6to4manual
subcommands.description: Use this command to set the 6 to 4 service such that it will get the relay address manually. Specify the <relayaddress> that you would like to set

args.name: newnetworkservicename
args.description: The name of the 6 to 4 service to be configured

args.name: relayaddress
args.description: The manually specified relay address to be used by the 6 to 4 service

subcommands.name: -getwebproxy
subcommands.description: Displays Web proxy (server, port, enabled value) info for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setwebproxy
subcommands.description: Set Web proxy for <networkservice> with <domain> and <port number>. Turns proxy on. Optionally, specify <on> or <off> for <authenticated> to enable and disable authenticated proxy support. Specify <username> and <password> if you turn authenticated proxy support on

args.name: domain
args.description: The domain name or IP address of the proxy server

args.name: portnumber
args.description: The port number for the proxy server

args.name: authenticated
args.description: Specify whether proxy authentication is required

args.name: username
args.description: The username for proxy authentication

args.name: password
args.description: The password for proxy authentication

subcommands.name: -setwebproxystate
subcommands.description: Set Web proxy on <networkservice> to either <on> or <off>

subcommands.name: -getsecurewebproxy
subcommands.description: Displays Secure Web proxy (server, port, enabled value) info for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setsecurewebproxy
subcommands.description: Set Secure Web proxy for <networkservice> with <domain> and <port number>. Turns proxy on. Optionally, specify <on> or <off> for <authenticated> to enable and disable authenticated proxy support. Specify <username> and <password> if you turn authenticated proxy support on

args.name: domain
args.description: The proxy server's domain or IP address

args.name: portnumber
args.description: The port number for the proxy server

args.name: authenticated
args.description: Specify whether proxy authentication is required

args.name: username
args.description: The username for proxy authentication

args.name: password
args.description: The password for proxy authentication

subcommands.name: -setsecurewebproxystate
subcommands.description: Set Secure Web proxy on <networkservice> to either <on> or <off>

subcommands.name: -getsocksfirewallproxy
subcommands.description: Displays SOCKS Firewall proxy (server, port, enabled value) info for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setsocksfirewallproxy
subcommands.description: Set SOCKS Firewall proxy for <networkservice> with <domain> and <port number>. Turns proxy on.  Optionally, specify <on> or <off> for <authenticated> to enable and disable authenticated proxy support. Specify <username> and <password> if you turn authenticated proxy support on

args.name: domain
args.description: The proxy server's domain or IP address

args.name: portnumber
args.description: The port number for the proxy server

args.name: authenticated
args.description: Specify whether proxy authentication is required

args.name: username
args.description: The username for proxy authentication

args.name: password
args.description: The password for proxy authentication

subcommands.name: -setsocksfirewallproxystate
subcommands.description: Set SOCKS Firewall proxy to  either <on> or <off>

subcommands.name: -getproxybypassdomains
subcommands.description: Displays Bypass Domain Names for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setproxybypassdomains
subcommands.description: Set the Bypass Domain Name Servers for <networkservice> to <domain1> [domain2] [...]. Any number of Domain Name servers can be specified. Specify "Empty" for <domain1> to clear all Domain Name entries

args.name: domain
args.description: Domains to bypass proxy for. Use 'Empty' to clear all bypass domains

subcommands.name: -getproxyautodiscovery
subcommands.description: Displays Proxy Auto Discover for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -setproxyautodiscovery
subcommands.description: Set Proxy Auto Discover for <networkservice> to either <on> or <off>

subcommands.name: -setautoproxyurl
subcommands.description: Set proxy auto-config to url for <networkservice> and enable it

args.name: url
args.description: The URL of the proxy auto-config (PAC) file

subcommands.name: -getautoproxyurl
subcommands.description: Displays proxy auto-config (url, enabled) info for <networkservice>

subcommands.name: -getairportnetwork
subcommands.description: Displays current Wi-Fi Network

subcommands.name: -setairportnetwork
subcommands.description: Set Wi-Fi Network to <network> using optional [password] if specified

args.name: network
args.description: The name of the Wi-Fi network to connect to

args.name: password
args.description: Optional password for the Wi-Fi network

subcommands.name: -getairportpower
subcommands.description: Displays whether Wi-Fi power is on or off

subcommands.name: -setairportpower
subcommands.description: Set Wi-Fi power to either <on> or <off>

subcommands.name: -listpreferredwirelessnetworks
subcommands.description: List the preferred wireless networks for <hardwareport>

subcommands.name: -addpreferredwirelessnetworkatindex
subcommands.description: Add wireless network named <network> to preferred list for <hardwareport> at <index>. Store the optional password in the keychain

args.name: network
args.description: The name of the Wi-Fi network to add

args.name: index
args.description: The position in the preferred list

args.name: securitytype
args.description: The security type of the network

args.name: password
args.description: Optional password for the network

subcommands.name: -removepreferredwirelessnetwork
subcommands.description: Remove <network> from the preferred wireless network list for <hardwareport>

args.name: network
args.description: The name of the Wi-Fi network to remove

subcommands.name: -removeallpreferredwirelessnetworks
subcommands.description: Remove all networks from the preferred wireless network list for <hardwareport>

subcommands.name: -getnetworkserviceenabled
subcommands.description: Displays whether a service is on or off (enabled or disabled)

subcommands.name: -setnetworkserviceenabled
subcommands.description: Use this command to turn the specified network service on or off (enable or disable)

subcommands.name: -createnetworkservice
subcommands.description: Create a service named <networkservice> on port <hardwareport>. The new service will be enabled by default

args.name: networkservicename
args.description: The name for the new network service

subcommands.name: -renamenetworkservice
subcommands.description: Use this command to rename the specified network service <networkservice> to <newnetworkservicename>

args.name: newnetworkservicename
args.description: The new name for the network service

subcommands.name: -duplicatenetworkservice
subcommands.description: Use this command to duplicate an existing network service <networkservice> and rename it to the specified name <newnetworkservicename>

args.name: newnetworkservicename
args.description: The new name for the duplicated network service

subcommands.name: -removenetworkservice
subcommands.description: Use this command to delete a network service <networkservice>. You cannot use this command to delete the last remaining service for a hardware port. To do so, you use the -setnetworkserviceenabled command

subcommands.name: -ordernetworkservices
subcommands.description: Use this command to designate the order network services are contacted on the specified hardware port. Name the network you want contacted first, then the second, and so on. Use "listnetworkserviceorder" to view current service order. Note: use quotes around service names which contain spaces (ie. "Built-in Ethernet")

subcommands.name: -setMTUAndMediaAutomatically
subcommands.description: Set hardwareport or device specified back to automatically setting the MTU and Media

subcommands.name: -getMTU
subcommands.description: Get the MTU value for hardwareport or device specified

subcommands.name: -setMTU
subcommands.description: Set MTU for hardwareport or device specified

args.name: value
args.description: The MTU value to set, typically an integer representing the maximum packet size

subcommands.name: -listValidMTURange
subcommands.description: List the valid MTU range for hardwareport or device specified

subcommands.name: -getMedia
subcommands.description: Show both the current setting for media and the active media on hardwareport or device specified

subcommands.name: -setMedia
subcommands.description: Set media for hardwareport or device specified to subtype. Specify optional [option1] and additional options depending on subtype. Any number of valid options can be specified

args.name: subtype
args.description: The media subtype to set for the hardware port or device

args.name: option
args.description: Additional options relevant to the <subtype>. Can include multiple values depending on the subtype

subcommands.name: -listValidMedia
subcommands.description: List valid media options for hardwareport or device name. Enumerates available subtypes and options per subtype

subcommands.name: -createVLAN
subcommands.description: Create a VLAN with the name <name> over the parent device <parentdevice> and with the tag <tag>

args.name: name
args.description: The name to assign to the new VLAN

args.name: parentdevice
args.description: The parent device over which the VLAN will be created

args.name: tag
args.description: The VLAN tag to assign to the new VLAN

subcommands.name: -deleteVLAN
subcommands.description: Delete the VLAN with the name <name> over the parent device <parentdevice> and with the tag <tag>

args.name: name
args.description: The name of the VLAN to be deleted

args.name: parentdevice
args.description: The parent device on which the VLAN is configured

args.name: tag
args.description: The VLAN tag of the VLAN to be deleted

subcommands.name: -listVLANs
subcommands.description: List the VLANs that have been created

subcommands.name: -listdevicesthatsupportVLAN
subcommands.description: List the devices that support VLANs

subcommands.name: -isBondSupported
subcommands.description: Displays YES if the device can be added to a bond. NO if it cannot

subcommands.name: -createBond
subcommands.description: Create a bond with the user-defined-name name and optionally add any listed devices if they support bonding

args.name: name
args.description: The user-defined name for the new bond

args.name: device
args.description: The devices to include in the bond. Any number of devices can be specified

subcommands.name: -deleteBond
subcommands.description: Delete the bond with the specified device-name

subcommands.name: -addDeviceToBond
subcommands.description: Add device to bond

subcommands.name: -removeDeviceFromBond
subcommands.description: Remove device from bond

subcommands.name: -listBonds
subcommands.description: List of all bonds

subcommands.name: -showBondStatus
subcommands.description: Display the status of the specified bond

subcommands.name: -listpppoeservices
subcommands.description: List all PPPoE services in the current set

subcommands.name: -showpppoestatus
subcommands.description: Display the status of the PPPoE service with the specified name

subcommands.name: -createpppoeservice
subcommands.description: Create a PPPoE service on the specified device with the service name specified

args.name: device
args.description: The network device to create the PPPoE service on

args.name: name
args.description: The name for the new PPPoE service

args.name: account
args.description: The PPPoE account name

args.name: password
args.description: The PPPoE account password

args.name: pppoeName
args.description: Optional name for the PPPoE service

subcommands.name: -deletepppoeservice
subcommands.description: Delete the service

subcommands.name: -setpppoeaccountname
subcommands.description: Set the account name for the service

args.name: account
args.description: The PPPoE account name

subcommands.name: -setpppoepassword
subcommands.description: Set the password for the service

args.name: password
args.description: The PPPoE account password

subcommands.name: -connectpppoeservice
subcommands.description: Connect the service

subcommands.name: -disconnectpppoeservice
subcommands.description: Disconnect the service

subcommands.name: -listlocations
subcommands.description: List all network locations

subcommands.name: -getcurrentlocation
subcommands.description: Display the name of the current set

subcommands.name: -createlocation
subcommands.description: Create a set with the user-defined-name name and optionally populate it with the default services

args.name: location
args.description: The name for the new location

args.name: populate
args.description: Specify 'true' to populate with default services or 'false' to create an empty location

subcommands.name: -deletelocation
subcommands.description: Delete the set

subcommands.name: -switchtolocation
subcommands.description: Make the specified set the current set

subcommands.name: -version
subcommands.description: Displays version of networksetup tool

subcommands.name: -help
subcommands.description: Displays a list of all the commands available in the Network Setup Tool, with explanatory information

subcommands.name: -printcommands
subcommands.description: Displays a list of commands with no detail
